title: Memory retrieval queries use junk keywords
root_cause: _prefetch_memory had no question-detection — it fired on statements, used the first non-stop-word regardless of specificity, and never normalised plural→singular. consciousness.txt query rules lacked a 'when NOT to search' decision step.
fix_code: _prefetch_memory: added RECALL_SIGNALS check so statements ('i followed up', 'im planning') return '' immediately. Expanded stop_words with generic nouns (location, things, planning). Added plural stripping: query.endswith('s') and len > 4 → strip trailing s.
fix_prompt: consciousness.txt: replaced 5-bullet query block with 3-step decision tree — Step 1 (is this a recall question?), Step 2 (proper-noun preference, filler rejection), Step 3 (singular retry on zero results).
test: testing/test_query_formulation_v2.py — costly, hits real Claude API
